element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Exterior military base with gradual enemy waves
New mechanic not in original Contra; demonstrated through terrain design

element_introduction_order.element: Weapon power-ups (machine gun, spread, bomb, laser)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Flying capsules destroyed to replace default gun; encourages experimentation
Resource management: pick same weapon twice for power boost
Eight-directional movement; perspective shift breaks side-scrolling rhythm
Overhead stages only; can store multiple; pressed via jump button
Appear between standard enemy waves to punctuate progression
Culminates each stage; increased difficulty from mid-bosses

typical_relief_triggers: End of side-scrolling segment transitioning to overhead view
typical_tension_triggers: Dense enemy waves in confined spaces
Perspective shift to unfamiliar overhead view
Inclined terrain requiring precise jump control
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating intensity: side-scrolling stages build linear pressure through enemy density; overhead stages shift pacing to multidirectional awareness; bosses create tension peaks; weapon acquisition provides rhythmic relief moments

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Flying capsules containing weapon upgrades encourage exploration and off-path shooting

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stages 1-2 introduce mechanics gradually with forgiving enemy placement; inclined surfaces and overhead perspective are tutorial moments
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 3-4 increase enemy density and introduce complex boss patterns; weapon selection becomes tactical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 5 combines overhead perspective with final alien lair; highest difficulty spike; boss has unused flashing mechanic suggesting originally harder design
notable_spikes: Second loop arcade version (difficulty maxed, no continues)

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages 1, 3, 4 use traditional left-to-right movement with enemy waves and mid-bosses punctuating advance. Inclined surfaces add verticality without breaking horizontal flow.
Stages 2, 5 shift to 8-directional movement from above. Breaks pacing of side-scrolling; players must recalibrate spatial awareness. Hyper shells provide screen-clear relief.
Each stage ends with final boss. Mid-bosses appear mid-stage. NES version reorders bosses from arcade; final stage features most complex encounter.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 5 serpent boss (has 4 possible paths, only 2 used)

notable_level_patterns.description: Default gun → first pickup replaces → second identical pickup upgrades. Encourages tactical choice and resource anticipation. Four weapon types support varied playstyles.
